name: Why I Am a Harper
author: Belbradyn Tralaer
description:
Belbradyn Tralaer's confessional explains why a successful merchant abandoned warehouses for coded songs and midnight rides — framed as advice to young idealists who confuse wealth with safety.

The final chapter lists debts Tralaer still owes in cities he can never visit openly again.

name: Waterdeep – An Elven Diary
author: Malenya Brighteyes
description:
Malenya Brighteyes records centuries of Waterdeep as seen from immortal patience — seasons of human folly, brief friendships, and the ache of outliving every garden she planted.

The diary's elven script is annotated in Common by a later reader who marked passages "still true" with a trembling hand.

name: Waterdeep
author: Unknown
description:
Waterdeep — A regional survey written for travellers who cannot afford to learn geography from dead companions. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Maps are absent or obsolete in most copies; readers are expected to update the text with their own ink.
